<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8" />
  <title>Tadashi Sushi | Experiencia Gourmet</title>
  <base href="/" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <link rel="icon" type="image/x-icon" href="favicon.ico" />
  <script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>
  <link href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.0.0/css/all.min.css" rel="stylesheet">
  <style>
      @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Montserrat:wght@300;400;700;900&family=Noto+Sans+JP:wght@300;400;700&display=swap');
      
      :root {
          --tadashi-red: #d90429;
          --tadashi-black: #0a0a0a;
      }

      body {
          font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif;
          background-color: var(--tadashi-black);
          color: #ffffff;
          scroll-behavior: smooth;
      }

      .font-jp { font-family: 'Noto Sans JP', sans-serif; }

      .logo-ring {
          position: relative;
          transition: all 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
      }

      .logo-ring:hover {
          transform: scale(1.1) rotate(5deg);
      }

      .logo-ring::after {
          content: '';
          position: absolute;
          inset: -4px;
          border: 2px solid var(--tadashi-red);
          border-radius: 50%;
          opacity: 0;
          transition: opacity 0.3s ease;
      }

      .logo-ring:hover::after {
          opacity: 1;
      }

      .card-sushi {
          transition: all 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.165, 0.84, 0.44, 1);
          background: linear-gradient(145deg, #111111, #050505);
          border: 1px solid #1a1a1a;
      }

      .card-sushi:hover {
          transform: translateY(-12px);
          border-color: var(--tadashi-red);
          box-shadow: 0 25px 50px -12px rgba(217, 4, 41, 0.4);
      }

      .active-tab {
          color: var(--tadashi-red);
          font-weight: 900;
      }

      .active-tab::after {
          content: '';
          position: absolute;
          bottom: -8px;
          left: 50%;
          transform: translateX(-50%);
          width: 24px;
          height: 4px;
          background-color: var(--tadashi-red);
          border-radius: 4px;
      }

      .no-scrollbar::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }

      .btn-glow {
          position: relative;
          overflow: hidden;
      }

      .btn-glow:hover {
          box-shadow: 0 0 25px rgba(217, 4, 41, 0.6);
          transform: scale(1.02);
      }

      .instagram-link {
          transition: all 0.3s ease;
      }
      
      .instagram-link:hover {
          background: linear-gradient(45deg, #f09433 0%, #e6683c 25%, #dc2743 50%, #cc2366 75%, #bc1888 100%);
          -webkit-background-clip: text;
          -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;
          transform: translateY(-2px);
      }

      @keyframes fadeIn {
          from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(20px); }
          to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
      }

      .animate-fade {
          animation: fadeIn 0.8s ease forwards;
      }
  </style>
</head>
<body class="bg-black">

</body>

**Finalizing XML Output**

I'm sure this will fix the compilation error in `src/components/notification.component.html`.

**Identifying Angular Issue**

I've pinpointed the issue: a JIT compilation error within an Angular app. The culprit seems to be the `@if` block, specifically the syntax in the `notification()` template reference variable. The error report indicates an unexpected "as" token in the expression related to notifications.


**Correcting `@if` Syntax**

The root cause now appears to be a subtle `@if` block syntax issue. I was initially focused on an incorrect "as" token, having fixed a similar problem previously. However, the Angular documentation confirms that the correct syntax for aliasing within an `@if` block is `(value; as alias)`, not `(value as alias)`. I'll implement this correction by replacing `as` with `; as` in the template file.


**Resolving `@if` Syntax**

I've confirmed the syntax mistake within the `@if` block. It needs a semicolon. The issue in `notification.component.html` is now clear: the code requires changing `(notification() as order)` to `(notification(); as order)`. This is a straightforward fix, directly addressing the JIT compilation error.


**Implementing Semicolon Fix**

I've determined the issue's source: the Angular `@if` block syntax. My earlier solutions were near misses. The correct form is `(value; as alias)`. The target file is `notification.component.html`. The faulty syntax, `@if (notification() as order)`, requires replacing "as" with "; as". This addresses the JIT compilation error stemming from the parser expecting a semicolon before the "as" keyword. I'm confident in this fix.

You've caught another subtle syntax issue! It seems my previous fix was incomplete. The new built-in control flow in Angular requires a semicolon before the `as` keyword when creating a variable alias.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8" />
  <title>Tadashi Sushi | Experiencia Gourmet</title>
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <link rel="icon" type="image/x-icon" href="favicon.ico" />
  <script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"></script>
  <link href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.0.0/css/all.min.css" rel="stylesheet">
  <style>
      @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Montserrat:wght@300;400;700;900&family=Noto+Sans+JP:wght@300;400;700&display=swap');
      
      :root {
          --tadashi-red: #d90429;
          --tadashi-black: #0a0a0a;
      }

      body {
          font-family: 'Montserrat', sans-serif;
          background-color: var(--tadashi-black);
          color: #ffffff;
          scroll-behavior: smooth;
      }

      .font-jp { font-family: 'Noto Sans JP', sans-serif; }

      .logo-ring {
          position: relative;
          transition: all 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
      }

      .logo-ring:hover {
          transform: scale(1.1) rotate(5deg);
      }

      .logo-ring::after {
          content: '';
          position: absolute;
          inset: -4px;
          border: 2px solid var(--tadashi-red);
          border-radius: 50%;
          opacity: 0;
          transition: opacity 0.3s ease;
      }

      .logo-ring:hover::after {
          opacity: 1;
      }

      .card-sushi {
          transition: all 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.165, 0.84, 0.44, 1);
          background: linear-gradient(145deg, #111111, #050505);

      }

          transform: translateY(-12px);
          border-color: var(--tadashi-red);

          color: var(--tadashi-red);
          font-weight: 900;

      .active-tab::after {
          content: '';
          position: absolute;
          bottom: -8px;
          left: 50%;
          transform: translateX(-50%);
          width: 24px;
          height: 4px;
          background-color: var(--tadashi-red);
          border-radius: 4px;
      }

      .no-scrollbar::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }

      .btn-glow {
          position: relative;
          overflow: hidden;
      }

      .btn-glow:hover {
          box-shadow: 0 0 25px rgba(217, 4, 41, 0.6);
          transform: scale(1.02);
      }

      .instagram-link {
          transition: all 0.3s ease;
      }
      
      .instagram-link:hover {
          background: linear-gradient(45deg, #f09433 0%, #e6683c 25%, #dc2743 50%, #cc2366 75%, #bc1888 100%);
          -webkit-background-clip: text;
          -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;
          transform: translateY(-2px);
      }

      @keyframes fadeIn {
          from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(20px); }
          to { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }
      }

      .animate-fade {
          animation: fadeIn 0.8s ease forwards;
      }

¡Excelente idea! He implementado un sistema completo para que puedas gestionar los pedidos y para que tus clientes puedan hacer un seguimiento de sus órdenes, manteniendo siempre su privacidad.

Aquí tienes un resumen de los cambios:

1.  **Nombre en el Pedido:** Al finalizar la compra, ahora se le pedirá al cliente su nombre para que puedas identificar fácilmente cada orden en la vista de "Cocina".
2.  **Nuevos Estados de Pedido:** He añadido el estado "En preparación". Ahora el flujo completo es: **Pendiente** → **En preparación** → **Listo para Retirar**. Cada estado tiene un color distintivo para una rápida visualización.
3.  **Vista de "Cocina" Mejorada:**
    *   Verás el nombre del cliente en cada ticket.
    *   Los botones de acción cambian según el estado del pedido, haciendo el proceso más intuitivo.
4.  **Nueva Sección "Mis Pedidos" para Clientes:**
    *   Añadí un botón "Mis Pedidos" en la navegación principal.
    *   Al hacer clic, los clientes pueden ver el estado en tiempo real de todos los pedidos que han realizado en su sesión, identificados por su nombre y el número de ticket (`#ID`), pero sin ver los pedidos de otros clientes.

Estos cambios le darán un toque mucho más profesional y organizado a la gestión de tu restaurante.
